rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 16, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT ANDREW GUILLORY'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS is OFF CALENDAR. Withdrawal of Motion on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAI D. GUPTA, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL VS. MEB LOAN TRUST II, A DELAWARE STATUTORY TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 16, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED because the moving party fails to shift its burden and there are triable issues of fact: Whether the notice caused Plaintiff prejudice; whether the mailed notice was in compliance with CC 2924b; plaintiff's remaining causes of action hinge on the wrongful foreclosure cause of action, including the quiet title, declaratory relief and the prayer for punitive damages. In addition motion for summary adjudication fails to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BOBBY CACANINDIN ET AL VS. ERNEST CANINDIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Possession
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 16, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF POSSESSION is DENIED. Pursuant to the Interlocutory Judgment referee has the power "to evict any person or person residing in the Real Property." Per CCP 873.070 referee is instructed to proceed with legally authorized eviction proceedings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: FRANK LUCCHETTI VS. NKECHI EMERUWA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 16, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND FOR SANCTIONS is GRANTED as follows: (1) no later than October 17, 2023 by 2:00 p.m. Defendant to produce all documents responsive to Request for Production included in September 11, 2023 Notice of Deposition by personal delivery or e-mail; (2) Defendant to appear for deposition via Zoom at 9:00 a.m. on October 18, 2023 and proceed with the deposition until Plaintiff concludes the deposition; (3) no later than October 19, 2023 by 9:00 a.m. Defendant to file a declaration attesting to good faith complete and timely compliance with this order; and (4) no later that October 19, 2023 by 2:00 p.m. Plaintiff may file a declaration addressing any deficiencies with Defendant's compliance. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
